content: Memory-efficient ICM implementations for large-scale curiosity-driven autonomous agents can be restructured to treat agent submodules as independent, curiosity-driven fine-tuning agents that only activate their intrinsic reward modules when encountering out-of-distribution state representations, cutting overall memory footprint by only storing forward/Inverse model parameters for active submodules rather than loading the full ICM at all times.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
